task: Implement member selection at project creation (plan: .omc/plans/ralplan-when-adding-new-project-user.md)

stories.title: Add withTransaction helper to lib/db.ts
acceptanceCriteria: lib/db.ts exports an async function withTransaction<T>(fn: (client: PoolClient) => Promise<T>): Promise<T>
Implementation uses getPool().connect(), issues BEGIN before fn, COMMIT after success, ROLLBACK on any throw, and releases the client in a finally block
lib/db.ts re-exports PoolClient from 'pg' for typing
TSDoc on the export warns: helpers using the module-level query() do not participate in the transaction; lists createProject as the current legitimate caller
pnpm tsc --noEmit returns 0 errors related to lib/db.ts

stories.title: Add hasProjectsDeadlineColumn probe + bulkInsertProjectMembers helper
acceptanceCriteria: lib/repositories.ts defines a module-level cached helper hasProjectsDeadlineColumn(): Promise<boolean> that queries information_schema.columns exactly once per process and memoizes the result
A code comment above the cache variable warns that schema migrations require a process restart and to not add hot-reload
lib/repositories.ts exports bulkInsertProjectMembers(client: PoolClient, projectId: string, userIds: string[]): Promise<void>
bulkInsertProjectMembers is a no-op when userIds.length === 0
bulkInsertProjectMembers uses SQL: insert into project_members (project_id, user_id) select $1, unnest($2::text[]) on conflict (project_id, user_id) do nothing
pnpm tsc --noEmit returns 0 errors related to lib/repositories.ts changes from this story

stories.title: Refactor createProject() to atomic transactional flow
acceptanceCriteria: createProject() in lib/repositories.ts accepts memberIds?: string[] in its args type
createProject() calls hasProjectsDeadlineColumn() before opening the transaction and picks the with-deadline or legacy INSERT variant based on the result

createProject() wraps the project insert + active-user filter SELECT + bulkInsertProjectMembers call in a single withTransaction(async (client) => ...) block; no try/catch column-missing fallback remains inside the transaction

Inside the transaction, memberIds is filtered to active users via select id, email from user_profiles where id = any($1::text[]) and is_legacy = false and email is not null using the transaction client
Creator (args.createdBy) is always present in the bulk insert set regardless of active-status filter result, preserving the historical invariant from lines 825 and 866
Both previous post-insert addProjectMember(created.id, args.createdBy) calls are removed
createProject() returns { project, skippedInactiveUserIds: string[], addedMemberEmails: string[] }
addedMemberEmails excludes the creator even if creator is active
skippedInactiveUserIds does not include args.createdBy

stories.title: Extend createProjectSchema and POST handler
acceptanceCriteria: createProjectSchema in app/projects/route.ts:10 includes memberIds: z.array(z.string().min(1)).max(200).optional()
POST handler destructures the new createProject return shape: { project: createdProject, skippedInactiveUserIds, addedMemberEmails }
POST handler dispatches emails via sendMail() AFTER setProjectStorageDir succeeds, NOT before; the existing Dropbox compensating deleteProjectById path is unchanged

Email dispatch is gated on config.emailEnabled() and uses Promise.allSettled over addedMemberEmails; each send is wrapped in .catch() that logs project_member_notify_failed with { projectId, email, error }
POST response shape is { project, warnings?: { skippedInactiveUserIds } }; warnings field is omitted when skippedInactiveUserIds is empty
A code comment in the handler documents the legacy-creator-skip edge case (creator inserted but absent from addedMemberEmails, absent from skippedInactiveUserIds)

stories.title: Wire member picker into create dialog UI
acceptanceCriteria: components/projects/projects-workspace-shell.tsx passes members, activeUsers, currentUserId, onAddMember, onRemoveMember props to ProjectDialogForm when opening the create dialog
activeUsers data is fetched and available in the create dialog (reuse existing fetch if one exists, otherwise add one)

components/projects/projects-workspace-context.tsx tracks memberIds: string[] in create-form state, exposes onAddMember/onRemoveMember handlers, and includes memberIds in the POST /api/projects submit payload
components/project-dialog-form.tsx accepts a currentUserId?: string prop
When rendering the member checkbox list, the row for currentUserId renders with both checked and disabled attributes; its onChange handler is a no-op
Submitting the form with the creator's checkbox does not include the creator's id in the submitted memberIds array (the server adds creator regardless)

stories.title: Client-side warnings toast for skipped inactive users
acceptanceCriteria: On successful create response containing warnings.skippedInactiveUserIds, the client resolves user names from the in-memory activeUsers list
A toast or inline message displays: 'Skipped: {names} — no longer active'
When response has no warnings field, no toast is shown

stories.title: Caller audit + project-wide checks
acceptanceCriteria: rg -n 'createProject\(' --type ts confirms app/projects/route.ts is the only direct caller of createProject; if other callers exist, they are updated to destructure the new return shape
pnpm tsc --noEmit returns 0 errors across the whole repo
pnpm lint returns 0 new errors (existing baseline preserved)

stories.title: Tests for new flow
acceptanceCriteria: A unit test exists asserting that bulkInsertProjectMembers is a no-op for [] (does not call client.query)
A unit test or integration test exists asserting createProject returns the new shape { project, skippedInactiveUserIds, addedMemberEmails }
A test verifies the route POST /api/projects accepts memberIds and returns warnings only when skipping occurred
pnpm test (or the project's existing test command) passes 100% (no new failures beyond preexisting baseline)
